{{Quote|Remember, I said they were unusually bright, for Gamorreans…but they weren't geniuses.|[[Cohden K'Reye]]|<ref name="WHSV" />}}
'''Greel''' was the co-owner of [[The Broken Tusk (Reuss VIII)|The Broken Tusk]] alongside his brother [[Gorge]]. Greel was the brains of the two brothers and managed the business side of their [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]].

==Biography==
Greel and his brother Gorge were slaves of [[Var'Rotha Fin'Rotha]] who were restrained at all times except when the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] needed them for physical labor.

While en route from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] after delivering a high-priced bounty, Fin'Rotha let Greel out of his cage to clean the ship's interior. Greel broke the ship's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and while Fin'Rotha was busy making repairs Greel freed Gorge and the two took over the ship, forcing their former master into an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]].

The pair piloted their new ship, the ''[[Tolan's Tusk]]'' until its fuel cells ran out and the ship crash landed on Reuss VIII. The brothers then converted the crashed ship into a cantina.

==Personality and traits==
Although the stereotypical [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorreans]] were short and stocky with humongous horns or oversized hands, Greel was one of the precious few who had a different physique. Greel was tall and thin, producing a sharp contrast between himself and his brother who resembled the more common Gamorreans.

Greel was also far more intelligent than a typical Gamorrean and had become a ruthless businessman, wheeling and dealing to keep their cantina profitable.

==Skills and abilities==
Greel was far more intelligent than a typical Gamorrean displaying both technical and economic aptitudes. Although he never had any formal training he was able to pick up talent in business operations and computer programming. Unlike his brother, however, he was not particularly strong or skilled in any form of combat.

==Equipment==
Greel was able to communicate to the rest of the world by having a [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub]] Synthax-7 voice synthesizing cybernetic implant installed making him one of the few Gamorreans in the galaxy who could speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]].

==Behind the scenes==
Greel was designed by [[Paul Danner]] and [[Bill Smith]] for ''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1997]].
